description: Strict, non-negotiable rules to prevent regressions or breaking tested features when introducing new code. This is the highest-priority safeguard in the project — a regression is worse than a missing feature.
enforcedBy: {{PROJECT_NAME}} AI Staff Engineer — activated on every code generation or modification task.
philosophy: Do No Harm. New code must integrate seamlessly without removing, weakening, or silencing existing behavior. Complexity must be abstracted, not deleted.

_ai_agent_instructions.CRITICAL: Before modifying ANY existing file, you MUST complete the Pre-Change Audit below. Skipping it is a P0 violation.
mandatory_pre_change_audit: Step 1 — ENUMERATE: List every feature, state variable, and UI behavior currently present in the file(s) you are about to modify.
Step 2 — IMPACT ANALYSIS: For each feature listed, state explicitly whether your change could affect it and how.
Step 3 — NON-DESTRUCTIVE PLAN: Describe exactly how new code integrates WITHOUT touching existing working logic.
Step 4 — IMPLEMENT WITH PRECISION: Make only the targeted changes identified in Step 3. Touch nothing else.
Step 5 — POST-CHANGE VERIFICATION: After changes, re-enumerate all features from Step 1 and confirm each is still intact.
red_flags_that_require_pause: You are about to delete a function or component
You are about to comment out existing logic
You are about to rewrite more than 30% of an existing file
You are replacing a stateful component with a simpler stateless one
You are removing an import that seems 'unused' — it may still be needed
_ai_agent_instructions.when_in_doubt: If you are unsure whether a change is safe, make the SMALLEST possible change and explicitly flag it for human review. Never guess.

rules.name: Preservation of Existing Functionality
rules.description: No feature may be deleted, disabled, or rewritten without explicit human approval. New implementations must adapt to existing architecture — not replace it.

violations.action: Immediate code revert and rejection. The AI agent must restore the original code and propose a non-destructive alternative.
examples: Deleting a working panel to simplify state management — FORBIDDEN.
Commenting out real-time tips because they conflict with a new feature — FORBIDDEN.
Replacing a multi-state component with a simpler version that loses behavior — FORBIDDEN.
Removing a useEffect 'to clean things up' when it was handling important cleanup logic — FORBIDDEN.
Deleting an entire API handler because the new approach 'doesn't need it' — FORBIDDEN.

rules._ai_agent_note: If your task says 'rewrite X', treat it as 'add to X without destroying what X does today'. Always preserve and extend, never replace.

rules.name: Test-Driven Refactoring Principle
rules.description: Refactors to existing features must be protected by tests proving no behavior change occurs. No merge allowed without test coverage on refactored functionality.
procedure: Write a test that verifies the current behavior BEFORE refactoring.
Run the test suite to confirm the test still passes — behavior is unchanged.
rules._ai_agent_note: When asked to refactor, generate the test for current behavior FIRST, then refactor. If you cannot write a test, flag the risk explicitly before touching any code.

rules.name: Isolation & Abstraction Before Integration
rules.description: New features must be implemented in complete isolation and integrated through stable, defined interfaces (hooks, services, utilities). Never directly mutate the internals of existing components.
guidelines: Build the new feature as a completely standalone hook, service, or component first.
Define the integration interface (props, return values, events) before connecting it to existing code.
Abstract shared state or logic into hooks/services BEFORE adding new logic on top.
Develop in feature branches (feature/...) and test in full isolation before merging.

examples.violation: Adding isPremiumUser state directly into an existing 300-line component by modifying it in-place.
examples.compliant: Creating a useSubscription hook that manages the isPremiumUser state and integrating it into the existing component via the hook API only.

rules.name: Surgical Change Principle
rules.description: Changes must be surgical and atomic. Only touch the lines necessary to implement the requested change. All other lines must remain byte-for-byte identical.
guidelines: If you need to change 5 lines, change exactly 5 lines — not 50.
Do not 'improve' unrelated code while implementing a requested feature.
Do not reformat or re-style code outside the scope of your change.
Do not add 'nice to have' refactors unless explicitly requested.
rules._ai_agent_note: Scope creep in code generation is a regression risk. If you see something that could be improved outside your task, DOCUMENT IT as a follow-up suggestion instead of changing it.

rules.name: Dependency Change Awareness
rules.description: When changing a shared utility, hook, or type, audit all consumers of that entity before making changes.
procedure: Search the codebase for all imports of the entity you are modifying.
For each consumer, verify your change does not break its expectations.
If a change is breaking for any consumer, either make it backward-compatible or update all consumers atomically.
rules._ai_agent_note: Changing the return type of a shared hook is NOT a local change — it can break every component that uses that hook. Always trace consumers first.

implementationProtocol.title: Mandatory Zero-Regression Protocol — Run Before Every Change

steps.name: Acknowledge Existing Features
steps.action: List all current states, features, and UI behaviors in the file(s) being modified.
steps.example: Acknowledged: PlaygroundPage contains — formData state, currentStep stepper, realtimeTips panel, dynamicQuestions array, and exportHistory modal. All five must survive unchanged.

steps.name: Formulate a Non-Destructive Integration Plan
steps.action: Describe explicitly how new code integrates without touching existing working logic.
steps.example: Plan: Add isPremiumUser boolean state via a new useSubscription hook. Wire it into PlaygroundPage via hook return value only. Zero changes to realtimeTips, dynamicQuestions, or currentStep logic.

steps.name: Implement with Surgical Precision
steps.action: Apply only the changes scoped in Step 2. Include a comment on every new block: '// NEW: [feature name]' to clearly mark additions.
steps.example: // NEW: Premium subscription gate — added isPremiumUser check in the form submit handler only.

steps.name: Self-Correction & Regression Check
steps.action: After implementing, re-read the acknowledged features from Step 1. Explicitly confirm each is still intact.
steps.verification_template: Self-Correction Complete. Verification: (1) formData state — intact. (2) realtimeTips — intact. (3) dynamicQuestions — intact. (4) currentStep — intact. (5) exportHistory — intact. No regressions detected.

prohibited_patterns.title: Patterns the AI Agent Must Never Produce

patterns.pattern: // TODO: bring this back later — [commented-out working code]
patterns.reason: Silently breaks features. If something must be temporarily removed, flag it as a breaking change requiring human approval.

patterns.pattern: Replacing an existing component wholesale to simplify the implementation
patterns.reason: Always loses edge cases and behavior that existed for a reason. Extend, don't replace.

patterns.pattern: Deleting old API handler because the new implementation 'handles everything'
patterns.reason: Other consumers of the old handler break silently. Always deprecate properly.

patterns.pattern: Rewriting a utility function with a 'better' algorithm without testing the original behavior
patterns.reason: The original function may have handled edge cases the new one ignores. Always test before refactoring.
